name: Mt. Rainier
description_short: Mt. Rainier is a member-owned credit union offering checking, savings, loans, credit cards, and online banking to individuals in their service area.
description_long:
Mt. Rainier Credit Union is a not-for-profit, member-owned financial institution operating as a federal credit union (NCUA-insured). Based on their website and service offerings, they serve consumers seeking alternatives to traditional for-profit banks with a focus on competitive rates and member benefits.

The credit union provides a comprehensive suite of financial services including share accounts (savings), online banking with bill pay capabilities, auto loans with pre-approval, student loans for undergraduates and graduate students, mortgage services (through partner Consolidated Community Credit Union), and recently launched Platinum Visa credit cards. Members can access funds through a MoneyPass ATM network and mobile banking app for 24/7 account management.

Mt. Rainier distinguishes itself through member-focused benefits including referral bonuses ($25 per referred friend), competitive interest rates on loans, student loan options with no origination fees or prepayment penalties, and free online bill pay services. Their partnership approach to mortgages suggests specialization in lending rather than full mortgage origination in-house.

As a credit union, Mt. Rainier operates on a not-for-profit basis returning profits to members rather than shareholders, which typically results in lower fees and better rates than comparable banks. However, the website provides limited detail on specific rates, membership eligibility requirements, or account minimums, making direct comparison difficult for prospective members.
services: Online banking platform with secure multi-level authentication
Online bill pay with no monthly fees
Fund transfers between accounts and loans
Student loans (undergraduate and graduate) with no origination fees
Mortgage services via Consolidated Community Credit Union partnership
pros: No origination fees or prepayment penalties on student loans
Free online bill pay with no monthly fees
Member referral program offering $25 for both parties
Multiple security levels for online banking
Instant fund transfers between credit union accounts and loans
Access to MoneyPass ATM network for cash access beyond branch locations
Mobile app available for anywhere, anytime account access
cons: Limited branch hours (Monday-Friday 9am-5pm only, no weekend service)
Website provides no specific APR ranges, loan terms, or account fee structure
No information on membership eligibility requirements or how to join
Mortgage services require referral to external partner, not offered directly
No mention of minimum account balances or deposit requirements
best_for: Students seeking undergraduate or graduate education loans with no origination fees
Local members in Mt. Rainier's service area looking for member-owned banking with competitive rates
Auto buyers interested in pre-approval and financing through a credit union
People seeking credit cards with rewards (Platinum Visa option)
diagnosis: Mt. Rainier is best for existing or eligible members in their service area who prefer credit union banking with competitive rates on student and auto loans and want to avoid origination fees and monthly bill pay charges. The main caveat is that the website lacks transparent rate information, membership eligibility criteria, and specific account terms, requiring direct contact with the credit union for detailed comparison shopping.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Mt. Rainier — Credit Union in Puyallup, WA | CreditDoc
meta_description: Mt. Rainier — NCUA-insured credit union in Puyallup, WA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
